repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover SDV6 Hybrid — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Only if caught very early via oil analysis
The 306DT is a one-shot engine — rebuild is rarely viable
Standard maintenance on any modern diesel
Only if under warranty or vehicle has high value

buyingChecks.title: Buying This SDV6 Hybrid? Check These First
buyIf: * Full JLR service history with documented oil analysis and 5,000-mile oil changes
* No cold-start knocking (critical — listen carefully on first start)
* DPF service history with evidence of regular regeneration (longer runs)
* Hybrid battery health check showing >85% state of health (or 8-year warranty still active)
* Evidence of regular longer journeys — not just short urban trips
avoidIf: * Cold-start knocking — the 306DT is a one-shot engine; a knock means budget for replacement
* No oil analysis history — assume the crank bearings are an unknown risk
* Unexplained electric range loss or charging errors — hybrid system may be compromised
* Short-trip ownership history — DPF and hybrid systems suffer from lack of use
* No specialist PHEV diagnostic capability locally — you'll need one

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The SDV6 Hybrid is a rare and complex machine — a diesel PHEV from a short production run. It combines the 306DT's known crankshaft bearing risk with a complex hybrid system. The 306DT is a "one-shot" engine — once the bearings fail, it's replacement time, not rebuild. With typical L405 values of £20,000–£40,000, a £6,500–£9,500 engine replacement is borderline but often viable. However, this is a vehicle for the brave — or for those with a comprehensive warranty. Due diligence on the crank bearings and hybrid battery is absolutely essential before purchase. If you're not prepared for the potential costs, choose the non-hybrid SDV6 or a petrol P400e instead.

cta.label: → Compare SDV6 Hybrid engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover SDV6 Hybrid (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start Knock — Listen carefully on first start; any knocking is a red flag for crank bearing issues
* Oil Analysis History — Request recent oil analysis reports; check for bearing metal contamination
* Service History — Including 5,000-mile oil changes and DPF maintenance — see the map above
* DPF Condition — Check for regeneration history; short trips without regen are a warning sign
* Electric Range — Compare displayed range against official figures; any major drop indicates battery degradation
* Charging System — Test both AC and DC charging; any errors or slow charging may indicate faults
* Battery Health — Request a battery health certificate (8-year warranty should guarantee 70% capacity)
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (DPF and NOx sensor issues are common)

items.question: How much does a Range Rover SDV6 Hybrid engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned SDV6 Hybrid engine typically costs £6,500–£9,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500 depending on the specialist. Used engines can be found from £3,500–£5,500, but condition varies dramatically. Note that battery replacement is a separate cost. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Range Rover.

items.question: Is the Range Rover SDV6 Hybrid engine reliable?
items.answer: The SDV6 Hybrid is a high-stakes ownership proposition. The 306DT diesel has a well-documented crankshaft bearing failure risk — a "one-shot" engine where failure means replacement, not rebuild. The hybrid system adds further complexity. However, with obsessive maintenance — 5,000-mile oil changes and regular oil analysis — some owners have achieved high mileage without issues. This is not a vehicle for the faint-hearted.

items.question: What are the common problems with the Range Rover SDV6 Hybrid?
items.answer: The main issues are crankshaft bearing failure on the 306DT diesel (cold-start knock is the early warning), DPF clogging exacerbated by PHEV duty cycles, and hybrid battery degradation over time. The combination of a high-risk diesel engine and complex PHEV system makes this the most demanding variant in the Range Rover lineup.

items.question: Is it worth replacing an SDV6 Hybrid engine, or should I scrap the vehicle?
items.answer: Given the typical L405 Range Rover value (£20,000–£40,000 for a good example), a £7,000–£9,000 engine replacement may be 20–40% of the vehicle's value — borderline but often viable for a cherished example. However, factor in battery health and the vehicle's overall condition. With potential for £15,000+ in combined engine and battery replacement costs, some owners choose to walk away.

items.question: How do I find my Range Rover SDV6 Hybrid engine code?
items.answer: The engine code is 306DT, found on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation. The hybrid system is integrated with the 3.0-litre six-cylinder diesel. If you're unsure, enter your registration on our site and we'll confirm it for free.

items.question: Can I repair a 306DT crankshaft bearing, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: The 306DT is a "one-shot" engine — once the bearings fail, the crank is typically scored and the block may be damaged. Rebuild is rarely cost-effective; replacement is the standard solution. Preventative oil analysis and 5,000-mile oil changes are essential to catch bearing wear before catastrophic failure.

faq.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network of engine specialists. Actual costs vary by variant, engine condition, damage severity and workshop location. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes.
